name: Invasion — Audio Debug
description: Single-renderer debug scenario: every `audio` event from 05_invasion, isolated for testing the audio popup renderer.

predefined_analysis_result.description: Maritime patrol acoustic buoy: heavy vessel engine signature at the RF contact position. Non-standard hull, no AIS transponder.
RCAF maritime patrol acoustic contact: heavy vessel engine signature. Non-standard hull profile, no transponder.
Halifax acoustic array: two-stroke piston hum — Shahed propulsion confirmed. 10–15 units.
Fredericton NB ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Northern acoustic buoy: propulsion signature confirmed, formation continuing toward Iqaluit.
Regina SK ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Ham radio St. John's NL: 'Dozens of them — like lawn mowers flying low and fast heading west. No lights.'
Explosion Iqaluit — North Warning System radar installation struck. Northern approach radar coverage degraded.
Saskatoon SK ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Iqaluit ham radio: 'The radar station is burning. We saw one drone, low, then the explosion. No warning at all.'
Thunder Bay ON ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Acoustic contact near Trois-Rivières: propulsion signature consistent with the earlier confirmed pattern.
Kelowna BC ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Explosion Montreal-Trudeau Airport — runway and downtown telecom exchange struck within seconds of each other.
Whitehorse YT ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Yellowknife NT ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Explosion CFB Bagotville — flight line struck. One of only two NORAD fighter-intercept bases in eastern Canada now offline.
Charlottetown PE ham radio net check-in: distant explosions and aircraft noise reported. Operator confirms this matches reports from other provinces on the network.
Explosion CFB Halifax — dockyard struck. Fires visible from across the harbour.
Explosion CFB Esquimalt — Pacific Fleet home dockyard struck.
Explosion — Shahed-136 warhead detonation downtown Toronto near Union Station.
St. John's ham radio: 'Halifax is being hit hard. We're hearing it on the marine band before it's even on the news.'
Victoria ham radio: 'Esquimalt dockyard is on fire. This isn't Ontario and BC anymore, it's everywhere at once.'
Whitehorse ham radio: 'I count twelve of them, all together, all heading for the airport and the tower.'
Ham radio near Trudeau: 'Soldiers everywhere on the tarmac. Montreal's biggest airport is gone.'
Explosion Prince Rupert — airport runway and port communications relay both struck within the same minute, all twelve contacts converging together.
Explosion Winnipeg — CFB Winnipeg / James Richardson Airport shared runway struck.
Explosion Ottawa — Macdonald-Cartier Airport. Radar installation destroyed. Control tower struck. Airport now non-operational. Pattern: both major Ontario airports hit within 20 seconds.

Explosion Whitehorse — Erik Nielsen Whitehorse International Airport struck. CBC North transmission tower hit within seconds, cutting territorial broadcast and much of the north's communications relay.
Explosion Kamloops — airport and regional telecom exchange struck. The Pacific wave has reached the BC interior.

Second explosion Ottawa — Vanier district, government communications node. Four strikes total in under 30 seconds: Union Station, Pearson Airport, Ottawa Airport, Ottawa comms hub. Coordinated suppression confirmed.
Explosion at Manitoba Hydro substation, Winnipeg -- part of the same coordinated infrastructure-denial pattern as the airport and telecom strikes.
Explosion CFB Trenton — CAF's primary air mobility hub struck. Runway and transport aircraft on the ground hit.
Explosion at Hydro-Québec substation, Montreal -- part of the same coordinated infrastructure-denial pattern as the airport and telecom strikes.
Explosion at BC Hydro substation, Victoria -- part of the same coordinated infrastructure-denial pattern as the airport and telecom strikes.
Explosion at NB Power substation, Moncton -- part of the same coordinated infrastructure-denial pattern as the airport and telecom strikes.
Chilliwack acoustic: heavy rotor noise — attack helicopter formation moving west over Fraser Valley ahead of tank column. Mi-28 or Ka-52 profile.
Explosion at NS Power substation, Halifax -- part of the same coordinated infrastructure-denial pattern as the airport and telecom strikes.
Ham radio near Winnipeg airport: 'Soldiers on the tarmac. Same as Toronto and Vancouver. They came in fast.'
Halifax acoustic: infantry column on foot, several hundred estimated, moving north out of the landing zone.
Ham radio near Calgary airport: 'They own the runway now. No fight, it was over in minutes.'
Explosion at Ontario grid node, Trenton -- part of the same coordinated infrastructure-denial pattern as the airport and telecom strikes.
Victoria acoustic contact: infantry column on foot moving north out of the landing zone toward the Malahat.
Ham radio from inside Pearson Terminal 1: 'Soldiers inside the airport. They came from helicopters on the runway. They are moving people out at gunpoint. Pearson is gone.'
Acoustic contact north of Halifax: drone formation reconning ahead of the column, standard pre-advance pattern.
Acoustic contact near Duncan BC: drone formation reconning ahead of the column, standard pre-advance pattern.
Ham radio near Elmsdale NS: 'Column of tanks and trucks heading up 102. Drones circling above them. We're getting out.'
Ham radio near Duncan: 'Tanks coming up the island highway. Drones ahead of them scouting. Everyone's heading for the ferry terminals.'
Ham radio from Richmond BC near YVR: 'Soldiers at the airport. It looks like the same thing they did at Pearson. They own the runway now. Vancouver airport is gone.'
Petawawa reserves: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Troops on foot flanking Truro via Robie Street, securing the perimeter ahead of the main column.
Edmonton garrison remnants: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Troops on foot flanking Nanaimo via the old Island Highway, securing the perimeter ahead of the main column.
Truro ham radio: 'They're not stopping. Column already reforming and moving toward the New Brunswick border.'
Gagetown NB reserves: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Nanaimo ham radio: 'Ferry terminal's gone quiet, they've got it. Column already moving toward Parksville.'
Kingston ON reserves: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Comox remnants: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Bagotville alert flight (if surviving aircraft remain): reporting status and attempting to establish contact with national command.
Cold Lake alert flight: reporting status and attempting to establish contact with national command.
Explosion Moncton — RCMP roadblock on Main Street struck by drone strike ahead of the column's arrival.
Valcartier remnants: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Explosion Parksville — RCMP roadblock struck by drone strike ahead of the column's arrival.
Trenton air mobility remnants: reporting status and attempting to establish contact with national command.
Acoustic Windsor Ambassador Bridge: tank engine and track clatter. Multiple units. Civilian traffic stopped.
Moncton ham radio: 'RCMP tried to hold the line, drones took them out before the tanks even arrived. Nothing left to stop them.'
Suffield AB reserves: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Parksville ham radio: 'RCMP tried to hold the line, drones took them out before the tanks even arrived. Same as everywhere else.'
Esquimalt naval remnants: reporting status and attempting to establish contact with national command.
Red Deer AB ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Windsor ham radio: 'Tanks crossing the bridge! Helicopters overhead covering their advance. Police are running.'
Borden ON reserves: attempting to mobilize remaining personnel and equipment despite earlier strikes. Command and control degraded but not eliminated.
Petawawa mechanized reserve: reporting status and attempting to establish contact with national command.
Kamloops BC ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Sept-Îles QC ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Shilo armoured reserve, MB: reporting status and attempting to establish contact with national command.
Sydney NS ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Barrie ON ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Acoustic Surrey Pacific Highway: tank signatures. Column crossing from Blaine WA. CBSA communications cut.
Kitchener ON ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Guelph ON ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Moncton ham radio — last transmission: 'Tanks on Main Street. Airport's gone. Maritimes are cut off from the rest of the country by air now.'
Parksville ham radio — last transmission: 'Tanks in the town centre. Airstrip's gone. Vancouver Island cut in half.'
Peterborough ON ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Brandon MB ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Lethbridge AB ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Medicine Hat AB ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Gander NL ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Sherbrooke QC ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Chilliwack BC ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Kingston ON ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Explosion at the CN Tower communications mast — broadcast and telecom relay capacity for the GTA degraded.
North Bay ON ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Sarnia: 400–600 troops in formation marching north. Helicopter circling overhead in overwatch.
Corner Brook NL ham radio: aircraft noise and distant explosions reported in the last hour, consistent with the national pattern.
Explosion at a downtown Toronto telecom data centre — regional internet exchange capacity affected.
Explosion Windsor near WFCU Centre — communications infrastructure struck. Power grid attack concurrent.
Explosion at the Vancouver port telecom exchange — port operations and shipping communications disrupted.
Explosion at a downtown Vancouver telecom data centre — same pattern as the Toronto strike.
Chilliwack radio: 'Tanks through downtown. Helicopters shooting at something north of town. Our town is behind them now.'
Explosion London near Victoria Hospital. Helicopter rocket strike on communications tower.
Explosion Vancouver — Second Narrows Bridge destroyed. Helicopter visible departing scene. North Shore isolated.
Explosions Burnaby SFU hilltop — helicopter rocket strike. High point seized.
Explosion Gardiner and Spadina — anti-tank engagement. Simultaneous helicopter strafing run on Canadian position.
Vancouver ham radio — last transmission: 'Tanks on Burrard. Soldiers rappelling from helicopters at False Creek. Tank guns firing downtown. Canada will endure. Good lu—' [cut]
